State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441

October 24, 1984


Steven Miller
State Field Director
Common Cause
636 So. Hobart Blvd., Suite 226
Los Angeles, CA 90005

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our No. A-84-252

Dear Mr. Miller:

I am in receipt of a copy of your letter to Roger Brown,
Director of our Enforcement Division. In that letter you
request that your pending advice request be treated as a formal
complaint by that Division. Therefore, I am transmitting to
Mr. Brown the materials which you forwarded with your advice
request.

To clarify my telephone conversations with you, we are
unable to provide specific advice under Government Code Section
83114 to your organization in this instance because Common Cause
had no "duty or obligation" under the Political Reform Act.
However, we were preparing a responsive letter which would have
discussed the requirements of the law in general terms. You
prefer a more focused response to the specific facts involved
and have, instead, elected to request an enforcement
investigation. That is your prerogative.

If we can be of further assistance in the future, please do
not hesitate to call on us.

Sincerely,

Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel
Legal Division

REL:plh
cc: Roger Brown
W. R. Holcomb, Mayor
San Bernardino

State of California

Memorandum

To    :    Barbara                                    Date  :  10/4/84




From  :  FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
            Jeanne

Subject :  Attached


         Steven Miller would like an opinion as to whether the
Costs for this literature constitute an expenditure.  It was
paid for, in part, by the City of San Bernardino.

CITY OF SAN BERNARDINO, CALIFORNIA

FOUNDED IN 1810

Will your Water Bills come down?

YES, in most cases. The City and East Valley Water Districts are working to
insure that SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA WATER CO. CUSTOMERS RECEIVE
NITRATE FREE, PURE WATER AT LOWER RATES. Also, there are finaliz-
ing an agreement whereby EAST VALLEY WILL LOWER ITS RATES BY
ABOUT 10% in exchange for high altitude well water from the city.

If you have another water company, there will be no change.

Will you get semi-weekly Street Sweeping of your
neighborhood?

YES. THE CITY STREET SWEEPERS CLEAN NEIGHBORHOOD STREETS
ON THE SAME DAY EVERY OTHER WEEK. Trees are also trimmed as
needed. The City will repave and mark streets twice as often as they are now

Will you be able to keep your Animals?

YES. City and County rules on the raising of Horses and Livestock are now
identical. Even where they are not the same CITY LAWS WILL ALLOW
YOU TO KEEP ANY ANIMAL YOU HAVE. However, pigs will be welcome
for just two more years.

Will your Rubbish bills go up?

NO. The same company will pick up your rubbish. They will be required to
lower their charge to $6.10 per month to pick up everything at the curb twice
a week. Current rates are from $7-$7.50.

Will your Address change?

PARTIALLY. Your street number will change to four digits. This will end the
confusion which police, fire, paramedics and the post office face between
City and County numbers. However, the post office will continue to deliver
mail to your old number.

Your Zip Code and Street Name will not change. New street signs will go up.
A Highland or Del Rosa designation can be used as long as you prefer it.

Will your neighborhood lose its Identity?

NO. Your area will continue to be known as Del Rosa, Highland, Arrowhead
Surburban Farms, Warm Springs, East Highlands, or Mountain Shadows. In
Highland, the post office designation stays the same. Two proposed city
council seats would give the people of the area the power to vote for their
own needs. This would also mean representation on all City Commissions.

Hernandez
attacks use
of $56,000

By LARRY HICKS
Sun Staff Writer

SAN BERNARDINO — Councilman Ralph Hernandez, the only elected official here opposing the city's attempt to annex Highland, has accused the San Bernardino Area Chamber of Commerce of misusing a $56,000 city grant to survey attitudes of Highland residents toward annexation.

City Attorney Ralph H. Prince and a consultant hired to conduct the survey said Wednesday the money has not been misused nor has any provision in the agreement between the city and the chamber been violated.

"I have gone to great lengths to ensure that the integrity of this (survey) is maintained," said John Husing, the chamber consultant conducting the survey.

Survey interviewers are instructed to remain neutral when distributing the survey questionnaires to Highland residents, Husing said. In fact, the interviewers also are Highland residents who survey the neighborhoods where they live, he said.

But Hernandez, who opposed the council decision in May to grant funds for the survey, said the study is tainted by the politicking of a Husing employee, Reggie Greene, a member of a black mens organization that recently endorse annexation.

Greene is a member of the Westside Action Group, which last month endorsed annexation. Her nandez said two members of WAG, whose names he would not reveal, told him Greene spoke in favor of annexation and the endorsement.

Greene said he abstained from voting on the endorsement. He attended the meeting as a member of WAG and not in his role for the chamber, Greene said.

The group decided to support annexation after hearing Hernandez and Councilman Daniel Frazier, who supports annexation, speak on the issue, said Ernest Wilson, WAG vice president.

The organization's executive board also reviewed the city's proposed plan for services to the Highland area before giving its endorsement, Wilson said.

"We looked at everything carefully," Wilson said. "I resent the implication that that wasn't done."

"Nobody manipulated anybody. The Westside Action Group has many strong-willed individuals, and they will not be manipulated," he said.

Hernandez also questioned Wilson's role in the vote because Wilson is the city's affirmative action officer and is answerable to Mayor V.B. Holcomb, who supports annexation.

"The mayor has never told me what to do," said Wilson.

SAN BERNARDINO AREA CHAMBER OF COMMERCE
546 W. 6TH ST. • P.O. BOX 658 • SAN BERNARDINO, CA 92402 • (714) 885-7515

August 3, 1984


Dear Chamber Member:

Your Chamber of Commerce needs your help!

Soon our community will face the most serious decision in its
history:

Should we unite under one local government?

In November the people of the unincorporated areas surrounding
the city will answer with their votes. Now, however, is a time
for facts, questions and reflection.

The chamber has agreed to play a major role in providing the
public with facts about what is being proposed and to serve as
a forum for questions and opinions.

As part of that process we need to know which of our members have
been affected area. It is our intent to set up a special
information system for you . . . and ask you to provide us with
opinions on what the city's top priority capital improvements and
services should be in the various parts of the unincorporated area.

Please fill out the enclosed card and return it to us at your
earliest convenience.

Sincerely,

[signature]
EUGENE WOOD
President

Funds voted
for Highland
attitude study

By ERIN KELLY
Sun Staff Writer

SAN BERNARDINO — Over the
strong objections of 3rd Ward
Councilman Ralph Hernandez, a
City Council committee voted
Wednesday to allocate $56,000 for
a Chamber of Commerce study to
assess the attitude of Highland
residents toward annexation.

In a related action, the Council
Committee on Relations with
Unincorporated Territories voted
2-1 to ask Highland voters to increase the council from seven to
nine members if annexation is
successful.

Hernandez called the chamber
allocation — which requires approval from the full council — a
misuse of public funds for political purposes and said the city's financing of the study will "contaminate it with politics."

The chamber's neutrality is
wiped out by this $56,000," Hernandez said. "We've hired a gun."

But 4th Ward Councilman
Steve Marks and chamber Executive Vice President William
Wood said it is common knowledge that the chamber supports
annexation.

"We've never been neutral,
Ralph," Wood said.

The chamber, which will put
$6,000 of its own funds into the
study, is proposing to do a neighborhood-by-neighborhood survey,
interviewing 8,000 people about
the kinds of public services they
want and the conditions under
which they would support annexation.

The study results will be presented in three reports, to be issued on June 15, July 15 and Sept.
1.

City leaders hope an annexa-

(Please see Study, B-8)

tion election will be conducted in November.

If we're going to have an election in November,
the people of Highland have a right to know all the
sides before they vote," Wood said. "And the work
has to be done now."

But Hernandez argued that the study will duplicate efforts already being done by other city and
county agencies and will just antagonize Highland
residents.

"They're tired of being studied," he said. "They
want to be left alone."

Responded Wood, "If they want to be left alone

)

(111)
Name _________________________________________________________________

Address ________________________________________________________________

SAN BERNARDINO AREA CHAMBER OF COMMERCE SURVEY

The purpose of this interview is to provide you with facts about
the impact on your home and neighborhood of joining the City of
San Bernardino. This interview is divided into topics. At the
end of each topic I will ask you if you have further questions.
If you do, I will try to answer them, or get answers for you.

TOPIC: TAXES

1. The first concern of most families is what joining the city
will cost. Did you know that under Proposition 13 YOU CANNOT
BE REQUIRED TO PAY ONE EXTRA PENNY IN PROPERTY TAXES IF YOU
JOIN A CITY?

CIRCLE: (YES    NO)

2. There is one new tax you would pay . . . the 8% utility tax.
So that I can estimate the monthly cost of this tax for you,
what is your typical:

a. Monthly Phone Bill                    __________

b. Monthly Electric Bill                 __________

c. Monthly Gas Bill                      __________

d. Cable TV Bill                         __________

ADD UP THE TOTAL                         __________

MULTIPLY TOTAL BY                        x .08

YOUR TAX WILL BE: ______________________ THIS IS YOUR MONTHLY
COST OF JOINING THE CITY.

3. There is an exemption from this tax for certain families.
How many people live in your home?

CIRCLE: (one    more than one)

IF ONE, READ: If your yearly income is under $9,000, you
don't have to pay this tax.

IF MORE THAN ONE, READ: If your yearly income is under
$12,000, you don't have to pay this tax.

10. Do you know where the nearest 12 hour City Police Community
Service Center would be located?

IF THEY DON'T KNOW READ: It is being put up at Sterling and
Highland. The city will expand from 39 to 63 uniformed off-
icers on duty East of Waterman Avenue if you join the city.

11. Do you have other questions about police protection?

a. _________________________________________________________________

_________________________________________________________________

b. _________________________________________________________________

_________________________________________________________________

TOPIC: WATER

12. What is the name of your water company?

IF IT IS EAST VALLEY WATER CO., READ: The City and East
Valley have agreed that the city will give East Valley
gravity flow water from the city's high altitude wells. East
Valley will thus save the electricity costs of pumping water
above ground. East Valley will pass along this cost savings
by lowering water rates about 10% to city residents.

13. Do you have other questions about water?

a. _________________________________________________________________

_________________________________________________________________

b. _________________________________________________________________

_________________________________________________________________

TOPIC: STREET MAINTENANCE

14. Do you know how often the city sweeps neighborhood streets?

CIRCLE: (yes     no)

IF NO, READ:  The city sweeps neighborhood streets once
every other week on the same day.

15. Do you know the city policy on tree trimming?

CIRCLE: (yes     no)

IF NO, READ:  Trees are trimmed for free on request and on a
regularly scheduled basis.

a. ________________________________________________________________

b. ________________________________________________________________

c. ________________________________________________________________

TOPIC: BUILDING PROJECTS

20. Do you want new curbs or gutters in your neighborhood?

CIRCLE: (yes     no)

IF YES, READ: Homeowners must pay the cost of these
improvements. If your family income is below $22,000 you
would qualify for zero interest loans for them.

IF NO, READ: This is not a requirement.

21. Do you want new street lights?

CIRCLE: (yes     no)

IF YES, READ: This is a project the city will eventually
build.

IF NO, READ: This is not a requirement.

22. Do you plan to build a new home?    CIRCLE: (yes     no)

IF NO, READ: There will be no change in your sewer
service or rates if you join the city.

IF YES, READ: You should contact the City Water Department
about fees.

23. The revenues the city will earn from the unincorporated
areas will be enough to provide the services outlined above
and leave about $400,000 for improvements in the area. Are
there specific important improvements (flood control, parks,
sidewalks, curbs, gutters, lights, etc.) that you want in-
stalled?

a. ________________________________________________________________

b. ________________________________________________________________

c. ________________________________________________________________

TOPIC: ADDRESSES

24. READ: If you join the city, your street name will remain the
same, but you will receive a new house number and new
signs identifying the street. The post office will
continue to deliver mail at your current number for
two years.

FPPC TRANSMITTAL OF CORRESPONDENCE

CORRESPONDENT [illegible] Common Cause                    DATE
                                                          REC'D 10/14/84

DATE 11/9/84    RE: [illegible] Outreach [illegible] is
                    an [illegible]

___LEGAL         ___ENFORCEMENT          ___TA&A
___BARBARA
___JANIS
_√_BOB L.
___DIANE

DATE REPLY DUE 11/23/84

ACKNOWLEDGMENT SENT _____

ADVICE NUMBER A-84-252

REQUEST SEI _____


                                    CALIFORNIA COMMON CAUSE
                                    (213) 387-2017

                                    Steven Miller
                                    State Field Director

                                    636 S. Hobart Blvd, Ste 226 • Los Angeles, CA 90005

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance · · Administration · · Executive/Legal · · Enforcement
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441

October 11, 1984

Steven Miller
State Field Director
California Common Cause
636 S. Hobart Blvd., Suite 226
Los Angeles, CA 90005

Re: A-84-252

Dear Mr. Miller:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to Robert E. Leidigh, an
attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at
(916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
General Counsel

BAM:plh

sections.question_synthetic: Whether costs for literature distributed by Common Cause regarding a San Bernardino annexation campaign, partially funded by the City of San Bernardino, constitute a reportable expenditure under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC was unable to provide specific advice under Government Code Section 83114 because Common Cause had no 'duty or obligation' under the Political Reform Act. The agency was preparing a general discussion of legal requirements but the requester elected to pursue an enforcement investigation instead.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains multiple components: formal advice letter from FPPC, internal memorandum, campaign literature, news articles, and survey materials. The core FPPC response is brief and indicates declination of specific advice with referral to enforcement. No formal Q/C sections with verbatim text found in main advice letter. The annexation context and literature examples are background materials attached to the request.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether costs for literature distributed by Common Cause regarding a San Bernardino annexation campaign, partially funded by the City of San Bernardino, constitute a reportable expenditure under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC was unable to provide specific advice under Government Code Section 83114 because Common Cause had no 'duty or obligation' under the Political Reform Act. The agency was preparing a general discussion of legal requirements but the requester elected to pursue an enforcement investigation instead.

embedding.summary: This FPPC advice letter (A-84-252) addresses Steven Miller's request regarding whether campaign literature costs constitute expenditures, with partial City of San Bernardino funding. The FPPC declines to provide specific advice but notes the matter has been referred to the Enforcement Division per Miller's request.
